name: Unidosus Counseling Connection (Ucc)
description_short: UnidosUS's free housing counseling service connects clients to community-based organizations via phone and virtual sessions for pre-purchase, foreclosure, and financial literacy counseling.
description_long: UnidosUS, formerly known as the National Council of La Raza, is the nation's largest Hispanic civil rights and advocacy organization with over 50 years of history. The organization operates through a unique network of nearly 300 community-based organization affiliates across the United States and Puerto Rico, combining research, advocacy, and direct programs to address social, economic, and political barriers affecting Latino communities. Unidos Counseling Connection (UCC) is a non-traditional call center operation based in Phoenix, Arizona that provides free housing and financial literacy counseling to clients. The service operates through telephone and virtual sessions, connecting clients with trained housing counselors from their affiliate network who deliver one-on-one guidance. UCC distinguishes itself by operating a hybrid workforce model where counseling is delivered remotely through a centralized call center, rather than traditional in-person office locations. The counseling services span pre-purchase education, foreclosure prevention and resolution, post-modification guidance, and broader financial capability training. This approach allows UnidosUS to scale services across their affiliate network while maintaining consistent quality standards and organizational mission alignment. The service is explicitly designed to serve Latino communities and those seeking to build financial stability, with bilingual Spanish-language capability preferred among counselors. A key caveat is that this is a job posting for hiring a housing counselor position rather than a direct consumer-facing marketing page, meaning specific pricing, availability, or enrollment procedures are not detailed in the available source material.
pros: Free services—no fees charged for housing and financial literacy counseling
HUD-aligned counseling covering pre-purchase, foreclosure prevention, and post-modification scenarios
Remote accessibility via phone and virtual sessions removes geographic barriers
Bilingual Spanish-language services available to serve Latino communities
Connected to network of nearly 300 community-based organizations providing coordinated support
One-on-one personalized counseling with budget development and written action plans
Established 50+ year track record through UnidosUS's civil rights and advocacy mission
cons: Limited consumer-facing information available—primary source is a job posting, not marketing materials
Requires phone or virtual access, not in-person counseling options
No clear information on wait times, appointment availability, or enrollment processes
Counselor qualifications require 2-5 years housing experience, but no transparency on current staffing levels
Service appears to route through affiliate network rather than direct UnidosUS provision, potentially affecting consistency
best_for: First-time homebuyers seeking pre-purchase education and mortgage readiness counseling
Homeowners at risk of foreclosure needing immediate prevention guidance
Spanish-speaking Latino households seeking culturally aligned financial guidance
Borrowers needing post-modification counseling after loan restructuring
diagnosis: UCC is best for Latino families and first-time homebuyers seeking free, bilingual housing counseling and foreclosure prevention support through a remote call center model. The main caveat is that this is a job posting rather than consumer-facing marketing material, so specific enrollment processes, wait times, and current availability require contacting UnidosUS directly.
services: Pre-purchase housing counseling and homebuyer education
Foreclosure prevention and counseling for at-risk borrowers
Post-modification counseling for borrowers after loan restructuring
Financial capability and financial literacy counseling
Budget development and financial goal-setting assistance
Written action plan creation supporting client financial objectives
Client motivation and accountability through follow-up support
Caseload management with ongoing client support throughout program duration
Financial and housing issue consultation and resource guidance
Telephone counseling delivery via call center model
Virtual counseling sessions via remote platforms
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 3003 N Central Ave Ste 610, Phoenix, AZ 85012
